**[65:39]** So in, uh, title 30, a main statute, specifically chapter 2 0 6, um, that's the statute that governs development districts are more specifically tax increment financing districts. And there's really two types. The, I call one of them the more traditional economic development type district. And then there's also affordable housing districts. But for all intents and purposes, they, they function very, very similarly. Um, t's one of the most impactful tools that municipalities have to leverage investment in public infrastructure, um, and also to, uh, attract development, um, through, um, tax incentives. **[66:26]** geographic boundaries, and that's important, um, as we'll get into in a second. So, um, how are TIF revenues created? So the first step, when a district is established, um, there is the then current assessed value of the underlying property, right? And so at the time the district is established, the value of the property is set, and we refer to that as the original assessed value of the parcel or the, or a grouping of parcels. And the taxes that are paid on that original assessed value, they continue to go on the general fund. So that effectively sets the baseline. Um, so step two is the incremental value. So that's, you know, uh, project X happens, um, $10 million in new value gets created

**[67:11]** because of the project. That's the incremental or the new value. And that is what is captured, um, by the, the TIFF district. Um, and that's called, referred to as the captured assess value. And so the taxes that are paid on the captured assess value, those are the TIFF revenues. Um, so in summary, uh, revenues are only created as a result of an increase in assessed value. How are the TIFF fund funds used? There are broadly three categories. So of the monies that are retained by the city, um, most often there's a sort of pay as you go model where each year as taxes are paid, the city retains those TIF dollars and they become essentially available within that TIF fund

**[67:57]** to spend on a whole range of municipal projects. And within the development program of each district, there's a table of, of eligible project costs that are approved by the legislative body. Um, so the second category is municipal bonds. **[69:30]** and sewer infrastructure and whatnot to start developing that, that municipal business park, it may be that debt needs to be incurred up front to build some of that infrastructure.
